steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks the student to ‘make and support a claim’ — a position about human arrival in North America backed by specific evidence from the notes. The notes document multiple pre-Clovis sites that contradict the Clovis First hypothesis.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — states the claim and cites evidence: asserts the Clovis were likely not the first humans, then backs it with items from more than a dozen sites suggesting earlier arrival — exactly what a supported claim requires. ✓
  • Choice B — asserts a position without naming evidence: makes a claim but provides no specific support from the notes. ✗
  • Choice C — merely restates the Clovis First hypothesis: takes no position against it and makes no claim. ✗
  • Choice D — references a single site vaguely: too narrow and too imprecise to constitute a well-supported claim. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
